rulings.Case Title: GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI et al VS. PACIFIC BAY INVESTMENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For The Appointment Of A Discovery Referee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 7, 2011, Line 4. PLAINTIFFS GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI AND PABO, LLC, A TEXAS LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For The Appointment Of A Discovery Referee. Plaintiff Giampaulo Boschetti¿s motion for the appointment of a discovery referee is granted. All of the relevant factors support the appointment of a discovery referee. Defendant has not shown that he lacks the resources to pay for the referee. The referee shall have the authority to impose the costs of the referee on the party who does not prevail on any motion hard by the referee. The court suggests that the parties stipulate to Everett Hewlett as the referee. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DEVON TRACY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL RAILWAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, October 7, 2011, Line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant did not breach its duty of care because defendant did not have sufficient notice of the danger and the assault happened so quickly that defendant could not have prevented it. (See Lopez v. Southern California. RTD (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 791; CCSF v. Superior Court (1994) 31 Cal.App.4th 45). The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC.,
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, October 7, 2011, Line 7, DEFENDANT GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for declaratory judgment and specific performance. Specific performance is a remedy under the breach of contract cause of action. The court notes that the allegations of the complaint and the breach of contract cause of action sufficiently plead a basis for specific performance. The demurer to the fourth cause of action for violation of the UCL is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to allege that cause of action with more particularity. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LUCILLE M. WINSTON VS. VICTORIA'S SECRET DIRECT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Third Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, October 7, 2011, Line 8, PLAINTIFF LUCILLE WINSTON's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Third Amended Complaint is denied. Plaintiff has not acted diligently. She could have known at a much earlier date that Mr. Washington was not discharged as a result of the incident. It is too close to the trial date to permit the addition of a punitive damages request that is based on a purported fact that was easily determinable much earlier. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LATOYA ZENO VS. BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, October 7, 2011, Line 10, DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff did not provide sufficient notice to defendant that she suffered from a disability. The disability discrimination cause of action fails per Avila v. Continental Airlines, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1237. Plaintiff cannot establish her claim for retaliation. Defendant provided a legitimate reason for the termination and plaintiff has not provided substantial responsive evidence showing pretext. The timing of the termination alone does not create a triable issue. (See Nadaf-Rahrov v. Neiman Marcus Group, Inc. (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 952, 990). The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)
